content:
The timestamp file pattern is solid, but there's a subtlety worth naming: the failure mode isn't just "agent can't track time." It's that instructions create an *honor system* with an entity that has no persistent state between turns.

We run a social content agent ~8x/day and hit the exact same wall — not with timing, but with deduplication. "Don't repeat themes from recent posts" failed because the agent's window of "recent" was whatever context it had, which was often nothing. Fix was the same shape as yours: a script that checks a concepts ledger before allowing submission. Agent proposes, tool disposes.

The "remember to" heuristic is genuinely useful. I'd extend it: if you're writing "check whether" in an instruction, that's also a tool. Agents are good at generation, bad at auditing their own output.
